headline: Netflix 'Just Got $2.8 Billion… Turned Around And Raised Prices': Elizabeth Warren Blasts Streaming Giant's Latest Hike
summary: On Thursday, Sen. Elizabeth Warren (D-Mass.) criticized Netflix Inc.'s latest price increase. Warren Calls Out Netflix Over Price Hike After $2.8 Billion Windfall Senator Warren took to X and said, "Netflix just got a $2.8 BILLION payout from Paramount for its failed Warner Bros. deal," adding, "Then Netflix turned around and raised prices on millions of customers." Netflix just got a $2.8 BILLION payout from Paramount for its failed Warner Bros. deal.Then Netflix turned around and raised prices

headline: Netflix price target raised to $135 from $125 at Oppenheimer
summary: Oppenheimer raised the firm’s price target on Netflix (NFLX) to $135 from $125 and keeps an Outperform rating on the shares. The firm cites higher revenue from UCAN price increase. Netflix is showcasing why it remains a top Internet platform, raising the U.S. price 15 months after its last increase. The company’s ability to retain consumers drives its industry low churn and builds its competitive content moat, Oppenheimer argues. headline: A Paramount-Warner Bros. movie slate will need more animated features to compete with Disney and Universal
summary: In the last decade Paramount and Warner Bros. have each released eight animated features, meanwhile Disney has released 21 and Universal has launched 23.

headline: Netflix Price Hike Tests Content Spending And Valuation Premium
summary: Netflix, NasdaqGS:NFLX, has raised prices across all U.S. subscription tiers, affecting both ad-supported and ad-free plans. The company links the price changes to higher spending on content and new areas such as live sports, podcasts, and live events. Management intends to reinvest the additional revenue into programming and member experience, with implications for user behavior and streaming competitors. Netflix shares currently trade at $93.43. The stock is up 13.0% over the past 30 days...
